claim: Motor neurons from patients with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis have electrophysiological changes relative to healthy patients.

result.claim: Motor neurons from patients with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis have electrophysiological changes relative to healthy patients.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by multiple pieces of evidence. Paper 17307849, "Intrinsic membrane hyperexcitability of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis patient-derived motor neurons," directly states that "hyperexcitability detected by clinical neurophysiological studies of ALS patients is recapitulated in induced pluripotent stem cell-derived motor neurons from ALS patients harboring superoxide dismutase 1 (SOD1), C9orf72, and fused-in-sarcoma mutations" (prop_3366, prop_3367, prop_3368). This paper also notes that "SOD1(A4V/+) ALS patient-derived motor neurons have reduced delayed-rectifier potassium current amplitudes relative to control-derived motor neurons" (prop_3370), which may be the underlying cause of hyperexcitability. Furthermore, it highlights that "Motor neurons produced from a genetically corrected but otherwise isogenic SOD1(+/+) stem cell line do not display the hyperexcitability phenotype" (prop_3369), reinforcing the link between ALS and these electrophysiological changes. Clinical neurophysiological studies of ALS patients have also detected hyperexcitability (prop_3365). Additionally, paper 37156706 (chunk_17846) reports that "in studies on human subjects with ALS the motor cortex was reported to be hyperexcitable." Paper 13919437 (chunk_17601, chunk_17600) also discusses complex changes in motor neuron excitability in an ALS mouse model. The consistency across these findings, including human iPSC-derived neurons and clinical observations, provides strong support for the claim.

evidence.text: SOD1(A4V/+) ALS patient-derived motor neurons have reduced delayed-rectifier potassium current amplitudes relative to control-derived motor neurons.
evidence.source: Intrinsic membrane hyperexcitability of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis patient-derived motor neurons.

evidence.text: Converting ApoE4 to ApoE3 by gene editing rescued the phenotypes of the neurons.
evidence.source: Gain of toxic Apolipoprotein E4 effects in Human iPSC-Derived Neurons Is Ameliorated by a Small-Molecule Structure Corrector

evidence.text: The Phase Response Curve (PRC) of brain and peripheral oscillators are remarkably similar.
evidence.source: Neural Network Interactions Modulate CRY-Dependent Photoresponses in Drosophila

evidence.text: Studies on human subjects with ALS reported the motor cortex to be hyperexcitable.
evidence.source: Reach task-associated excitatory overdrive of motor cortical neurons following infusion with ALS-CSF

evidence.text: Induced pluripotent stem cell-derived motor neurons from ALS patients harboring superoxide dismutase 1 (SOD1) mutations display hyperexcitability.
evidence.source: Intrinsic membrane hyperexcitability of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis patient-derived motor neurons.
